# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What this project is

`cafl4ds` — **Continuous, Active Federated Learning for Data Streams**. A research project studying a *coupled
active-learning loop*: an active-learning filter acts as a label-free streaming selector over a self-supervised (SSL)
backbone that is trained via federated learning over transient data streams, with a representation-health monitor that
re-aims selection.

**The codebase is currently an early scaffold** (generated by PyMaxQ). `cafl4ds/__init__.py` is empty, the only runtime
code is the Hydra example in `scripts/example.py`, and tests are placeholders. The real architecture is the *research
design*, not yet-written code:

- **`docs/project-plan.md` is the spec.** It defines the experiment structure future code must implement — the streaming
    loop, the filter families (the "knobs": novelty F-a, coverage F-b, steerable F-c), SSL backbones (MAE /
    joint-embedding), the health-metric suite (RankMe/effective-rank, drift, forgetting), the FL track, baselines, and a
    phased plan. Read it before designing anything non-trivial. The novelty claims are tagged `N-A`… `N-G`; new work
    should map to one of them or it is scope drift.

## Environment & dependencies (uv)

Managed by **`uv`**. Python `>=3.10` (the synced `.venv` uses 3.12).

```bash
source .env              # only if pulling from the private GitLab registry (see below)
uv sync --group dev      # creates .venv, installs project editable + dev tools
```

Private-registry auth (optional): create a git-ignored `.env` with `UV_INDEX_<PACKAGE>_USERNAME` /
`UV_INDEX_<PACKAGE>_PASSWORD` (a GitLab PAT with `read`).

Run anything in the env with `uv run <cmd>` (no need to activate). Versioning is **git-tag-based** via `hatch-vcs` (no
hardcoded version; fallback `0.0.0`).

## Common commands

Tasks are defined as **`poe` (poethepoet)** tasks in `pyproject.toml`; run with `uv run poe <task>`.

| Command                                                          | What it does                                                                  |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `uv run poe test [cpus] [paths...]`                              | pytest (`-n cpus`, default 1) over `test/unit`, then coverage xml/html/report |
| `uv run pytest test/unit/test_dummy.py::test_dummy`              | **run a single test** (pytest directly)                                       |
| `uv run poe clean`                                               | remove caches, coverage, `docs/exported`, `__pycache__`, build dirs           |
| `uv run poe docs [--verbose]`                                    | build mkdocs site (run `poe test` first — docs embed coverage/test badges)    |
| `uv run poe bump-version-tag <patch\|minor\|major> [--annotate]` | create the next git version tag                                               |
| `uv run poe get-latest-tag` / `poe docs-version`                 | latest remote tag / publish versioned docs via `mike`                         |

`pytest` is preconfigured (see `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`) to always run coverage and emit
`docs/exported/pytest.{html,xml}`, with live logging at DEBUG. `testpaths = test`.

## Linting, formatting, type-checking

Config lives in `pyproject.toml`; the canonical runner is **pre-commit**.

```bash
uv run pre-commit install          # do this once before committing
uv run pre-commit run --all-files  # run every hook
```

Direct invocations: `uv run ruff check` (auto-fixes; `fix = true`), `uv run ruff format`, `uv run mypy cafl4ds`. Line
length is **120**. Ruff lint is strict (includes bandit `S`, bugbear `B`, pyupgrade `UP`); `test/*` is exempt from
`S101` (asserts). Docstrings follow the Google convention. `docs/` is excluded from ruff/mypy.

**Heads-up:** the `no-commit-to-branch` pre-commit hook blocks direct commits to the default branch — work on a feature
branch. `check-added-large-files` caps additions at 1 MB.

## How code is meant to be written here

- **Hydra-driven, instantiation-based.** Entry points are `@hydra.main` scripts (see `scripts/example.py`) reading
    configs from `cafl4ds/configs/` (root config `main.yaml`). Objects are built with `hydra.utils.instantiate`, so
    components should be plain classes wired via config (`_target_: ...`), not hardcoded. Run with overrides, e.g.
    `uv run python scripts/example.py key=value`. Hydra writes run dirs to `outputs/` (ignored).
- **Logging** uses `loguru`.
- Package code goes in `cafl4ds/`; experiment/CLI entry points in `scripts/`; tests in `test/unit/`. Only `cafl4ds` is
    packaged into the wheel.

## Docker

- `docker/Dockerfile` — multi-stage production image. **Requires `--build-arg VERSION=...`** (hatchling reads it via
    `SETUPTOOLS_SCM_PRETEND_VERSION`; there is no `.git` in the build context). Private deps and internal CA certs are
    passed as build secrets — see the header comment in the file.
- `docker/ci.base.Dockerfile` — base image for CI (uv + poethepoet + certs).

## Claude Code in this repo

This project expects Claude Code to run behind a token-compression pipeline (RTK shell-hook + Headroom API proxy) — see
`docs/claude.md`. Practically: bash output you see may be RTK-filtered, and `.claudeignore` already excludes `.venv`,
`uv.lock`, caches, and generated artifacts from indexing.
